behavior: Inquisitive, acrobatic foliage-gleaner; often hangs upside down while taking insects from leaves and bark. Typically forages in social flocks outside the breeding season and carries food to a perch to handle it.
field_marks: Tiny, round-bodied bird with a large head and short neck
Black cap and neat black throat bib
Stark white cheeks separating cap and bib
Soft gray back, wings, and long narrow tail
Short, pointed dark bill; dark eyes and gray legs
habitat: Deciduous and mixed woodlands, swamps and streamside woods, open woods, parks, and tree-rich suburban or urban settings.
